Full-stack data map

DATA FLOW — lines = data moving (page ↔ IndexedDB ↔ server).

Legend

Vertical position = what the code actually does (read from its data edges, not its folder). Bottom→top: the page (reader.blade.php) ▸ code that bridges page↔IndexedDB ▸ the IndexedDB object stores ▸ code that bridges IndexedDB↔server ▸ PostgreSQL tables.

Horizontal column = source folder (labelled across the top, and by colour): indexedDB, hyperlights, hypercites, divEditor. So each box's column is its folder and its row is what it does. A box sitting in a row that doesn't match its folder's natural role (e.g. a hyperlights file up in the IndexedDB-store row) = code acting out of role — a candidate to move when restructuring.

Single-click anything to trace its connections in the current lens — the rest dims but stays visible so you keep your place. Double-click a module box to drill into its files (and again to collapse).

The show code coupling button flips the whole map to a second lens: instead of data flow, lines become which function calls which (the code's internal wiring). Orange lines there = a call crossing folders — modules reaching into each other, i.e. tight coupling worth untangling.
